xDiagnosticsRegistry.canonicalMessage: The design-system adapter manifest or a reachable adapter contract is invalid.
The adapter manifest does not exactly reach every adapter, source lock, and locked source file.
A design-system adapter grants or points to executable implementation authority.
An executable catalog, compiler, or ingestion path is not registered by architecture policy.
An executable catalog, compiler, or ingestion route is not registered by architecture policy.
An adapter-data change includes a path outside the closed data-and-derived-output allowance.
A registered command-line route is absent from executable code.
A command-line route is absent from the closed architecture inventory.
An executable uses a nonliteral dynamic import or CommonJS require.
An executable reads the P2 catalog or evidence path outside the frozen direct-consumer exceptions.
A frozen direct P2 consumer exception no longer matches an executable string literal.
A registered executable path is absent from the scanned repository.
An executable path is absent from the closed architecture inventory.
A v0 architecture guard, execution-control file, or causal mutation changed outside a versioned migration.
A branch-free shared module uses an identity-bearing field in control flow, comparison, regex, or concatenation outside the closed generic allowances.
The candidate policy grows a frozen legacy exception set relative to the base policy.
A frozen legacy executable changed instead of shrinking through deletion and retirement.
A frozen P0 or P2 import exception no longer matches the executable implementation graph.
A P0 or P2 implementation import is outside the frozen legacy exception set.
A local executable import does not resolve inside the scanned implementation graph.
The immutable migration baseline does not match its architecture-policy hash.
An architecture mutation fixture did not produce its exact expected diagnostic.
Architecture policy sets must be sorted and unique.
The architecture policy does not satisfy its closed machine contract.
The candidate architecture policy weakens an admitted base or its fail-closed structural protections.
A protected shared module imports a local dependency outside the protected branch-free closure.
An admitted protected-module edge is not present in the executable implementation graph.
A local import touching a protected shared module is not an exact admitted edge.
A protected shared module is absent from the executable implementation graph.
A branch-free shared module contains a source-system or component identity literal.
